No Poverty.reason: The course covers international trade rules and specifically discusses "Developing countries", which relates to creating policy frameworks (Target 1.b) to support poverty eradication actions through trade.

Zero Hunger.reason: The course explicitly covers "SPS measures" (food safety), "Tariff & QRs", and "Subsidies", which directly relate to Target 2.b on preventing agricultural trade restrictions.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course covers the "Intellectual Property (TRIPs)" agreement, which is central to Target 3.b concerning access to affordable essential medicines and public health flexibilities.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course on "International Economic and Trade Law" is fundamentally about the rules governing economic growth (Target 8.1) and specifically discusses "Developing countries" (Target 8.a).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course examines trade rules that shape industrialization and market access (Target 9.2, 9.3) and covers "Intellectual Property (TRIPs)", which relates to technology and innovation (Target 9.b).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course focuses on the WTO and has a dedicated topic on "Developing countries", directly addressing Target 10.a (special and differential treatment) and 10.6 (representation).

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The topic "Standards & safety: TBT & SPS measures" can cover environmental regulations and eco-labeling, which are tools for more sustainable production patterns (inferred link to Target 12.a).

Climate Action.reason: Topics like "Subsidies" and "Border measures" are relevant to trade disputes over green technologies and carbon border adjustments, linking to integrating climate measures into policy (Target 13.2).

Life Below Water.reason: Target 14.6 explicitly mentions the "World Trade Organization fisheries subsidies negotiation", which falls directly under the course topic "Trade remedies: Antidumping, Subsidies & Safeguards".

Life on Land.reason: The course covers "Intellectual Property (TRIPs)" (relevant to Target 15.6 on genetic resources) and "Border measures" (relevant to Target 15.7 on combating trafficking of protected species).

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course is centered on the WTO's "rule-based approach for the solution of trade disputes", directly supporting Target 16.3 (international rule of law) and 16.6 (effective institutions).
